title: Change blueprint — implementation plan
question: Will this plan build the accepted specification safely?
lede: Gate B reviews how the accepted spec becomes code.
gateQuestion: Approve this plan as the build contract?

specContext.acceptedVia: accepted in chat after Gate A review
specContext.summary: One-paragraph restatement of the accepted scope and non-goals.

slicesLede: Vertical slices; every slice leaves the tests green.

slices.title: First independently green slice
slices.detail: What lands and how it is proven.

slices.title: Second slice
slices.detail: What lands and how it is proven.

slices.title: User-visible wiring
slices.detail: The slice where behavior becomes visible.

flow.lede: State the dependency direction the plan preserves.
flow.caption: Explain what solid and dashed edges mean here.

riskyCode.lede: The single trickiest surface in the plan, shown as real code.

risks.risk: A real failure mode of this plan
risks.mitigation: How a slice or test bounds it.

rollout: how the slices reach users safely
rollback: the single step that undoes the change

options.label: Approve the plan
options.consequence: Implementation is authorized against this exact slice list.

options.label: Request changes
options.consequence: The plan reopens with your notes.

options.label: Reject the plan
options.consequence: The spec stands; this build approach is discarded.

recommendation.reason: State why, grounded in the slices and risks above.

openStatusLabel: Gate B open — no resolution proposed yet
